question: What did Mark Zuckerberg say about the future of mid-level software engineers and AI?
answer: Mark Zuckerberg suggested on The Joe Rogan podcast that within five years, AI might replace mid-level software engineers. This statement caused panic among many because it implies a significant reduction in software engineering jobs due to AI advancements.

question: How do I personally view the claim that AI will replace all mid-level software engineers within five years?
answer: I disagree with the claim that AI will completely replace all mid-level software engineers within five years. While AI tools are advancing and can assist with mundane tasks, I believe the role of software engineers will evolve rather than disappear. Software development involves complex problem-solving and team dynamics that AI cannot fully replicate yet.

question: How is AI currently changing the way software engineers work, according to my experience?
answer: I use AI tools mainly to handle mundane tasks or to guide me when I don't know how to do something. AI helps me save time by automating repetitive work, but I always review its output carefully. The role of software engineers is shifting towards higher-level thinking, understanding product requirements, and debugging complex issues rather than just coding simple tasks.
